taxdiv 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • Getting Started
    • Introduction to taxdiv
    • taxdiv Paketi Kullanim Rehberi (Turkce)

Calculate Ozkan's pTO Using the Original Excel Macro Formula

Source: R/ozkan_pto_macro.R
ozkan_pto_macro.Rd

Computes pTO using the weighting scheme from the original Ozkan Excel macro (TD_OMD). This differs from ozkan_pto() in how slicing steps are weighted: the macro uses only the number of "active" steps (steps where >= 2 species survive) rather than n_s = max_abundance as described in the published paper.

Usage

ozkan_pto_macro(community, tax_tree)

Arguments

community

A named numeric vector of species abundances. Names must match the first column of tax_tree.

tax_tree

A data frame with taxonomic hierarchy. First column is species names, subsequent columns are taxonomic ranks from lowest to highest (e.g., Species, Genus, Family, Order, Class, Phylum, Kingdom).

Value

A named list with components:

uTO

Unweighted taxonomic diversity (macro formula)

TO

Weighted taxonomic diversity (macro formula)

uTO_plus

Unweighted taxonomic distance (same as paper)

TO_plus

Weighted taxonomic distance (same as paper)

Ed_levels

Deng entropy at each taxonomic level (nk=0 slice)

Details

The Excel macro (TD_OMD, Ozkan 2018a) counts the number of slicing steps where the Deng entropy product is non-zero (n_active), then assigns weights n_active, n_active-1, ..., 1 to those steps only. Steps where 1 or fewer species survive are excluded entirely.

In contrast, ozkan_pto() follows the published formula (Ozkan 2018, Eq. 4) which uses n_s = max_abundance for all steps, including single-species steps (with product = 1 by the postulation rule).

The two functions give identical results when all slicing steps have 2 or more surviving species (n_active == n_s). They differ when some steps reduce to 1 or fewer species, which typically occurs in species-poor or abundance-skewed communities.

This function is provided for backward compatibility with results generated by the original Excel macro. For new analyses, use ozkan_pto() which implements the published formula.

Note

Maximum 9 slicing steps, matching the Westhoff & van der Maarel (1973) abundance scale used in the original macro.

References

Ozkan, K. (2018). A new proposed measure for estimating taxonomic diversity. Turkish Journal of Forestry, 19(4), 336-346. DOI: 10.18182/tjf.441061

See also

ozkan_pto() for the published paper formula, deng_entropy_level() for the core Deng entropy calculation.

Examples

comm <- c(sp1 = 4, sp2 = 2, sp3 = 3, sp4 = 1, sp5 = 2)
tax <- data.frame(
  Species = paste0("sp", 1:5),
  Genus   = c("G1", "G1", "G1", "G2", "G2"),
  Family  = c("F1", "F1", "F1", "F1", "F1"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
ozkan_pto_macro(comm, tax)
#> $uTO
#> [1] 2.795401
#> 
#> $TO
#> [1] 3.473158
#> 
#> $uTO_plus
#> [1] 3.060705
#> 
#> $TO_plus
#> [1] 3.753852
#> 
#> $Ed_levels
#>  Species    Genus   Family 
#> 1.609438 2.280003 0.000000 
#>
